title: Scatter Add
description:
Given values $v\in\mathbb{R}^n$ and integer links $\text{link}\in\{0,\dots,j-1\}^n$, return a length-$j$ vector where entry $p$ is the sum of all `values[k]` whose `link[k]==p` (scatter-add into $j$ buckets).

**Shapes**: `values`:[n], `link`:[n] -> output:[j].
starter_code:
import torch

def scatter_add(values, link, j):
    # values: [n], link: [n] ints in [0, j)
    # TODO: out[link[k]] += values[k], length j
    pass
hints: This is like bincount but weighted: build a `[j, n]` boolean matrix comparing every bucket `arange(j)` against `link`.
Matmuling that mask matrix with `values` instead of a ones vector sums the linked values per bucket — 1 line, broadcasting only.

solutions.name: Broadcast equality + weighted matmul
solutions.explanation: `arange(j)[:, None] == link[None, :]` gives a `[j, n]` matrix that is $1$ at $(p,k)$ exactly when $\text{link}_k=p$. Matmuling with `values` sums the weights routed to each bucket $p$, which is exactly a scatter-add, without ever indexing on the left-hand side of an assignment.
